summary:
The patient likely has \boxed{\text{Vitamin K Deficiency}} due to its impact on multiple coagulation factor activation processes leading to extended PT and APTT tests results despite stable levels of fibrinogen and platelets. This condition disrupts essential steps required for blood clots formation crucially impacting external as well internal bleeding risks. To confirm this hypothesis further testing such as measuring prothrombin time response after administration of exogenous vitamin k should be considered next step if not already done so clinically feasible within current context provided by available data set constraints. 

The summary maintains focus strictly upon core diagnostic implications derived from laboratory findings pertinent towards identifying underlying cause behind observed abnormalities noted during routine screening procedures conducted routinely across various medical settings including emergency departments or primary care clinics where prompt identification can lead timely interventions potentially saving lives especially when dealing acute hemorrhagic episodes requiring immediate attention . This condition disrupts essential steps required for blood clots formation crucially impacting external as well internal bleeding risks. To confirm this hypothesis further testing such as measuring prothrombin time response after administration of exogenous vitamin k should be considered next step if not already done so clinically feasible within current context provided by available data set constraints.
